outbound_official.note: No official Brunei government outbound visa-list page located. The MFA (mfa.gov.bn) Visa-Information page covers only inbound visa requirements for foreign nationals entering Brunei. The MFA Travel page explicitly directs Brunei citizens to check destination-country embassies or foreign government websites for visa requirements. The immigration.gov.bn site covers only inbound travel documents and passes. No mfa.gov.bn or immigration.gov.bn page lists visa-free/visa-on-arrival/eVisa/ETA destinations for Brunei ordinary-passport holders. Rely on destination-side data.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Category C1-CS30 – Visa on Arrival, up to 30 days. Confirmed on MFA index (C1) and Brunei embassy pages.
Category C4 – Visa on Arrival, up to 14 days; Single or Multiple Entry Visa; Fee: BND20.00/BND30.00

visa_on_arrival.nationality: Kuwait (Ordinary, Diplomatic and Special passport holders)
visa_on_arrival.notes: Category C1 – Visa on Arrival for Ordinary, Diplomatic, and Special passport holders, up to 30 days; Single or Multiple Entry Visa; Fee: BND20.00/BND30.00. Official passport holders are Category D2 (visa-free 30 days).
Category C3-CE14 – Visa on Arrival, up to 14 days
Category C2-CM30 – Visa on Arrival (single entry), up to 14 days; Fee: BND20.00. Confirmed on official MFA C2 page.

visa_policy.notes: Brunei's MFA classifies countries into 4 categories: A and B require advance visa from Brunei missions abroad; C is visa on arrival (sub-categories C1=30 days, C2=14 days, C3=14 days, C4=14 days); D is visa-free (sub-categories D1=90 days, D2=30 days, D3=14 days). No e-visa system was found on official pages. Some D3 entries apply to diplomatic/official passports only while ordinary passports fall under Categories A or B. Russia and China have visa-free access for ALL passport types under D3. Kuwait has a split arrangement: Ordinary/Diplomatic/Special passport = C1 VoA (30 days); Official passport = D2 visa-free (30 days). Iran and Morocco ordinary passports require a visa (Category A and B respectively); their diplomatic/official passports are D2 visa-free (30 days).

conditional_access.conditions: Category D2 – no visa required for diplomatic/official/service passports only, up to 30 days. Ordinary passport holders require a visa (Category A).
Category D2 – no visa required for diplomatic/official passports only, up to 30 days. Ordinary passport holders require a visa (Category A). Confirmed via Brunei MFA overseas mission pages.
Category D2 – no visa required for Official passport holders, up to 30 days. Ordinary and Special passport holders are Category C1 (Visa on Arrival, 30 days).
Category D2 – no visa required for diplomatic/official/service/special passports only, up to 30 days. Ordinary passport holders require a visa (Category B). Confirmed on official MFA index page.
Category D2 – no visa required for diplomatic/official passports, up to 30 days; ordinary passport holders are Category D3 (14 days). Also covered under ASEAN visa exemption framework.
Category D3 – no visa required for Diplomatic and Official passport holders only, up to 14 days. Ordinary passport holders require a visa (Category B).
Category D3 – no visa required for diplomatic/official passports only, up to 14 days. Ordinary passport holders require a visa (Category B).
Category D3 – no visa required for diplomatic/official passports only, up to 14 days. Ordinary passport holders require a visa (Category B).
Category D3 – no visa required for Diplomatic and Official passport holders only, up to 14 days. Ordinary passport holders require a visa (Category A).
Category D3 – no visa required for Diplomatic, Official, and Service passport holders only, up to 14 days. Ordinary passport holders require a visa (Category B).
Category D3 – no visa required for diplomatic/official passports only, up to 14 days. Ordinary passport holders require a visa (Category B).

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program exists in Brunei. Citizenship can only be acquired through extended lawful residence (generally 15 years of permanent residence, or 10 years if married to a Bruneian citizen). No official government source advertises a CBI programme.

programs.name: Long-Term Business Visit Pass
programs.notes: Introduced effective 31 December 2024. For company owners and foreign investors who meet economic contribution criteria, including job creation for locals and tax compliance. Valid up to 5 years with multiple-entry. Excludes convenience stores, restaurants, laundries, salons, and non-registered companies. No minimum investment amount stated on the official page. Pass may be revoked for law violations or failure to provide economic benefits. Does not explicitly lead to permanent residence.

programs.name: Long-Term Professional Visit Pass
programs.notes: For foreign experts and professionals whose expertise is beneficial and needed for Brunei's development. Granted based on national needs determined by relevant government bodies. Duration up to 5 years. No minimum salary or investment amount stated on the official page.

programs.name: Long-Term Social Visit Pass
programs.notes: For foreign nationals with family ties to Brunei citizens or permanent residents who do not yet meet criteria for permanent residency. Duration up to 5 years.

programs.name: APEC Business Travel Card (ABTC/KUPA)
programs.eligibility: Brunei citizens categorized as business people; requires letter of support from Ministry of Finance and Economy, Ministry of Primary Resources and Tourism, Brunei Chamber of Commerce, or Brunei Young Entrepreneurs Association
programs.notes: ABTC (KUPA) holders have access to fast-track immigration lane at Brunei International Airport. Card is valid for 5 years. First application fee: BND$100.00; Renewal: BND$50.00. This is an outbound facilitation card for Bruneian citizen business travellers, not an inbound visa scheme.

validation_notes: ADDED Iran to conditional_access: D2 visa-free 30 days for diplomatic/official passports (ordinary = Category A, visa required). Was entirely missing from previous record.
ADDED Morocco to conditional_access: D2 visa-free 30 days for diplomatic/official/service/special passports (ordinary = Category B, visa required). Was entirely missing from previous record.

CORRECTED Egypt in conditional_access: passport_types updated from ['diplomatic', 'official'] to ['diplomatic', 'official', 'service'] to match official MFA index page which lists Egypt diplomatic/official/service under D2-D30.
VERIFIED all visa_free (D1/D2/D3) and visa_on_arrival (C1/C2/C3/C4) entries against live mfa.gov.bn/Pages/Visa-Information.aspx — no other additions or removals required.
VERIFIED rbi Long-Term Pass programs still live and unchanged on immigration.gov.bn as of June 2026.
VERIFIED cbi has_program=false — no CBI programme found on any official Brunei government source.

NOTE: Saudi Arabia C2 category label corrected in notes from 'C (C2-CM30)' to 'C2-CM30' for clarity; max_stay_days 14 days confirmed (the code 'CM30' in the URL refers to the page name, not the stay duration).
gaps: Exact minimum investment amounts for Long-Term Business Visit Pass are not published on the official immigration.gov.bn page
Permanent Residency program eligibility criteria and financial thresholds are not publicly detailed on official immigration pages
No e-visa or ETA system found for Brunei on official government websites
Category A and B countries (visa required) were identified from the main MFA index page but individual country pages only link to generic Category A/B pages without additional per-country conditions

No official Brunei government source (mfa.gov.bn, immigration.gov.bn) publishes any conditional entry rule granting visa-free or VoA access based on holding a third-country visa (US, UK, Schengen, Australian, Canadian, Japanese, GCC), residence permit, permanent residence card, or OCI status. Brunei's policy is purely nationality + passport-type based.

Bilateral visa waiver/suppression agreement dates for the diplomatic/official passport exemptions (India, Bangladesh, Pakistan, Mongolia, Tajikistan, Thailand, Egypt, Iran, Morocco, China, Russia) are not published on official MFA or immigration pages.

Iran conditional_access: it is unclear from the MFA index page alone whether Iran 'service' passport also qualifies for D2 or falls under Category A alongside ordinary. Only 'diplomatic' and 'official' confirmed.
